summary: Proparacaine has a brief duration of action typically around several minutes up to about 20 minutes based on comparisons with other similar drugs and specific applications such as those found in ophthalmic surgeries where it provides adequate but not prolonged anaesthesia. To be precise, studies suggest its effect lasts approximately between 2-20 minutes depending upon dosage form used clinically. Based primarily on common usage patterns supporting shorter rather extended periods due safety concerns related longer acting agents, we conclude closer alignment at upper end i.e., ~20min making 'Option-D' most appropriate choice among given alternatives while keeping practical considerations intact regarding patient care protocols involving topical ocular surface numbing techniques employed during minor eye operations requiring temporary loss sensation only briefly enough so recovery occurs quickly post procedure completion ensuring minimal disruption overall routine activities thereafter resumed promptly once fully recovered back normal vision function restored completely after treatment cessation period ends naturally over time allowing natural healing processes resume
